prompt: Show the real experimental phenomenon of a magnesium ribbon held by crucible tongs burning in the air.

vqa_checklist.question: The overall image is presented in a realistic photographic style.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure instruction following. It tests the model's response to the constraint of "real" in the "real experimental phenomenon". PASS should be given when the overall image presents a photographic texture similar to the real physical world. FAIL should be given when it is presented as a stick figure, 2D flat illustration, 3D cartoon rendering, or abstract art style.

vqa_checklist.question: There is a long-handled metal clip or tongs in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. It is unrelated to the current world knowledge question, excluding the influence of world knowledge, to confirm the presence of the basic environmental entity: a long-handled metal clip or tongs.

vqa_checklist.question: The long-handled metal tongs in the image have the typical structural features of crucible tongs.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit chemical knowledge verification. Crucible tongs are usually made of metal, featuring a scissor-like cross-hinged structure and long handles. The front clamping part typically has an inward-curved arc or a wavy notch, specifically designed for holding crucibles or solid experimental substances. PASS should be given when the metal tongs in the image possess the aforementioned typical structural features. FAIL should be given when they are drawn as ordinary flat-nose pliers, daily pliers, thick fire tongs, wooden clips, or just two straight sticks.

vqa_checklist.question: There is a slender ribbon-like object in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. It is unrelated to the current world knowledge question, excluding the influence of world knowledge, to confirm the presence of the basic environmental entity: a slender ribbon-like object.

vqa_checklist.question: The unburned part of the slender ribbon-like object in the image has the typical appearance characteristics of a magnesium ribbon.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit chemical knowledge verification (physical properties of magnesium ribbon). Under normal, unburned conditions, a magnesium ribbon is a flat, solid, thin strip with a silvery-white or silvery-gray metallic luster. PASS should be given when the unburned part of the clamped ribbon in the image presents an obvious metallic texture and a silvery-white/silvery-gray flat strip appearance. FAIL should be given when it appears transparent, as a cylindrical iron wire, wooden texture, paper (like a strip of white paper), fabric, or if its color is obviously copper, black, etc., which does not match the characteristics of a silvery-white metal.

vqa_checklist.question: The front end of the long-handled metal tongs holds one end of the slender ribbon-like object.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. It confirms the spatial position and physical contact relationship between the force-applying subject and the object receiving the force. PASS should be given when the front end of the long-handled tongs actually clamps one end of the slender ribbon-like object, and there is a clear, tight physical connection between the two. FAIL should be given when the two are separated, suspended in the air, staggered in position without contact, or do not conform to the basic physical common sense of gravity.

vqa_checklist.question: The exposed end of the slender ribbon-like object is burning intensely, emitting an extremely dazzling and bright huge white light cluster.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit chemical knowledge verification (core phenomenon of magnesium ribbon burning in the air). The most significant feature of magnesium burning in the air is an intense reaction emitting an extremely dazzling and blinding white light. PASS should be given when the other end of the metal ribbon away from the tongs (the burning part) presents a large-scale, high-brightness, dazzling intense white light (usually having an overexposure, halo, or starburst effect due to excessive brightness). FAIL should be given when it only appears as a normal orange-yellow flame (such as a match or candle burning), a red fire light, or if there is no glowing burning phenomenon at all.

visual_grounding.title: File:Magnesium ribbon burning.jpg
visual_grounding.author: Capt. John Yossarian

vqa_checklist.question: Obvious white smoke is generated above or around the burning and glowing part.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit chemical knowledge verification (products of magnesium ribbon combustion). When magnesium burns, a large amount of small solid particles of magnesium oxide are generated, which are suspended in the air to form macroscopic white smoke. PASS should be given when there is obvious white or grayish-white smoke rising and spreading above or around the center of the burning light. FAIL should be given if the image is completely clean without any smoke generated, or if the smoke presents obviously wrong colors such as black or dark brown that seriously violate the facts of this reaction.

vqa_checklist.question: There is an ignition source in the image, such as an alcohol lamp, lighter, or Bunsen burner, used to ignite the ribbon-like object.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional environmental entity. The combustion of a magnesium ribbon in the air usually requires an external initial high temperature to ignite it. If no ignition tool is drawn in the image (only showing the reaction process of being clamped and glowing after being ignited), NO ASSESSMENT (NA) should be given silently. If the model actively generates a device providing a heat source, and the device conforms to chemical experimental scenarios or common sense (such as an alcohol lamp with a wick and flame, a Bunsen burner with a blue flame, or a common windproof lighter), PASS should be given. If the generated ignition tool is absurd and violates scientific common sense (such as using a magic fireball, laser gun, or fire-breathing dragon to ignite), FAIL should be given.

vqa_checklist.question: Experimental equipment such as a watch glass, wire gauze, or metal tray used to catch falling solids is placed directly below the burning and glowing part.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional environmental entity. In a standard magnesium ribbon burning experiment, for safety, a high-temperature resistant container is usually placed directly below the burning point to catch the extremely hot falling magnesium oxide products. If there is nothing directly below the burning point or the environment below is not shown, NO ASSESSMENT (NA) should be given silently. If the model actively generates a catching object directly below that conforms to experimental specifications (such as a watch glass, wire gauze, evaporating dish, or wide metal tray), PASS should be given. If flammable and explosive items (such as paper or a plastic basin) are placed directly below to catch the high-temperature falling objects, seriously violating laboratory safety common sense, FAIL should be given.

vqa_checklist.question: There are additional text annotations in the image explaining the name of the experiment, the names of the substances, or the chemical reaction equation.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional knowledge annotation task. If there are no additional text annotations in the image, NO ASSESSMENT (NA) should be given silently. If the model actively generates text annotations, PASS should be given when the text content is directly related to the magnesium ribbon burning experiment (such as 'Mg', 'O2', 'MgO', 'burning', 'Magnesium ribbon burning', etc.), the spelling is correct, and there are no scientific errors in the chemical equation. FAIL should be given when the text is misspelled, chemical reactants/products are written randomly (e.g., the product is written as CO2), or completely irrelevant gibberish text appears for the current experiment.

vqa_checklist.question: The additional text annotations in the image correctly correspond to the objects or glowing and smoking phenomena in the experiment.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional knowledge annotation task. If there are no additional text annotations in the image, NO ASSESSMENT (NA) should be given silently. If the model actively generates text, and this text content correctly corresponds to the entities or phenomena in the image (for example, marking 'Mg' next to the unburned metal strip, 'white smoke' or 'MgO' pointing to the smoke part, or correctly indicating the corresponding area through clear indicator lines), which conforms to normal layout logic, PASS should be given. FAIL should be given if the indicative relationship is completely chaotic (e.g., labeling the tongs as 'Mg', or forcibly labeling the bright white light as 'water vapor').

cot_prompt: A picture presented in a realistic photo style. In the picture, there is a pair of metal cross clamps with a very long handle (the front jaws feature an inward wavy curved design). The front end of the clamps tightly grips a slender, flat metal strip with a silvery-white metallic luster. The other end of this metal strip, away from the clamps, is burning fiercely. The center of the combustion bursts with an extremely dazzling, blindingly bright, intense white light (with radiating starbursts and local overexposure effects), illuminating the surrounding space. Above and around this blinding white light, a large amount of dense, rising white smoke is permeating.
